description_long: This object is a classic submachine gun with a robust metal receiver and barrel, complemented by wooden furniture on the stock and foregrip. The weapon features a prominent round drum magazine mounted beneath the receiver, characteristic of early 20th-century designs. The stock is made of wood, smoothly contoured for shoulder support, while the foregrip is vertical and ribbed for enhanced grip. The barrel is cylindrical and extends forward with a simple muzzle, lacking any visible flash suppressor or compensator. Iron sights are mounted along the top, with a rear notch and a front post, providing basic aiming capability. The overall finish is a combination of dark metal and brown wood, giving it a utilitarian yet iconic appearance.
description: A vintage submachine gun with a wooden stock, vertical foregrip, and a large drum magazine, featuring dark metal and brown wood finishes.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length, wooden stock, vertical foregrip, and drum magazine clearly.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular drum magazine and the alignment of the barrel and iron sights.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the wooden stock, drum magazine, and foregrip, with similar details as view 0.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the stock and the back of the drum magazine, with the iron sights visible along the top.
attribute_stock_design: fixed wooden stock with smooth contour
attribute_iron_sight_configuration: simple notch rear sight with fixed front post
